title: Baingan Bharta

ingredients.title: For the Bharta Masala
items: 2–3 green chilies, finely chopped (adjust to taste)
1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
Fresh coriander leaves, chopped for garnish

ingredients.title: Optional Garnish / Flavor Boost
items: 1–2 tsp butter or ghee (for extra richness)
1 small tomato, finely diced (for raw freshness on top)

instruction.title: 1. Roast the Eggplant
steps: Wash and pat dry the eggplants.

Roast them directly over an open flame (gas stove) or in a broiler/oven at 220°C (425°F) until the skin is charred and the flesh is soft (about 15–20 mins on flame; 30–35 mins in oven, turning occasionally).
Let cool slightly, then peel off the skin.
Mash the flesh thoroughly with a fork or potato masher until smooth and slightly fibrous.
Pro Tip: Roasting on an open flame gives that smoky, authentic flavor.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the Bharta Masala
steps: Heat oil or ghee in a pan over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds. When they splutter, add onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add green chilies and ginger-garlic paste; sauté until raw smell disappears.
Add chopped tomatoes, turmeric, chili powder, and coriander powder. Cook until tomatoes are soft and oil starts to separate (~8–10 mins).
Add the mashed eggplant and salt. Mix well and cook on low heat for 5–7 mins, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala and give a final stir.

instruction.title: 3. Garnish and Serve
steps: Drizzle with a teaspoon of ghee or butter for richness.
Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

chefTips: Smokiness: For maximum smoky flavor, pierce the eggplant with a fork and hold it directly over the gas flame for a few minutes before roasting fully.
Texture: Mash the eggplant by hand rather than in a blender to maintain a fibrous, rustic texture.
Flavor Depth: Slow-cook the tomato-onion masala until oil separates—it builds a rich, layered flavor.
Optional Twist: A teaspoon of roasted cumin powder or smoked paprika can elevate the aroma for fine dining.
